name: Apple-Cheese Corn Bread
recipeIngredient: Yellow or white cornmeal (1 cup), preferably stone-ground
Sharp cheddar cheese, shredded (1 and 1/2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, baking powder, salt, sage, mace, and cayenne. Stir well to blend.
2. Add cheese and chopped apples to the dry ingredients. Toss until evenly coated.
3. Set aside.
4. In a separate bowl, whisk together milk, water, egg, and baking soda.
5. Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
6. Melt butter in an oven-safe skillet (preferably cast iron) over medium heat until hot.
7. Add melted butter to the batter and gently stir until blended.
8. Return the batter to the skillet.
9. Sprinkle remaining cheese on top.
10. Bake at 375°F (190°C) until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean, about 25 minutes.
11. Remove from oven and let cool for 5 minutes before cutting into wedges and serving hot. Makes 8 to 10 servings.
